LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT FITNESS ANYWHERE LLC (TRX)'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FALSE LIGHT, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SLANDER AND CONSEQUENTLY IS SUPERFLUOUS. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MISREPRESENTATION IN VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SECTION 1050, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE THE STATEMENTS AT ISSUE WERE MADE IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR COMPETITION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AND CROSS-PLAINTIFFS BEAVERFIT NORTH AMERICA, LLC, ALEXANDER ROODHOUSE AND MICHAEL TAYLOR'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR NO. 9176, EMAIL: GEALESR@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT FITNESS ANYWHERE LLC (TRX)'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action for false light, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The second cause of action is duplicative of the first cause of action for slander and consequently is superfluous. As to the third cause of action for misrepresentation in violation of Labor Code Section 1050, the demurrer is overruled. The first amended cross complaint does not allege the statements at issue were made in anticipation of litigation. As to the fourth cause of action for unfair competition, the demurrer is sustained and cross-plaintiffs BeaverFit North America, LLC, Alexander Roodhouse and Michael Taylor's request for leave to amend is granted. Contrary to their arguments in opposition to the demurrer, cross-plaintiffs have not alleged TRX violated the spirit of the Unfair Practices Act by attempting to set up a monopoly and do not link the allegations to any specific constitutional, statutory, or regulatory provision. Nor do the cross-plaintiffs allege they actually relied on the allegedly false statements. As to the fifth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Cross-plaintiffs have not now, but potentially could allege that TRX is intending to continue actions injurious to defendant. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Application For Stay Of Proceedings Pursuant To Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert F. Kramer, of Dentons US, LLP, Tel. 650.798.0356, for defendants Beaverfit Ltd., appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANT BEAVERFIT NORTH AMERICA, LLC, ALEXANDER ROODHOUSE, MICHAEL TAYLOR'S Application For Stay Of Proceedings Pursuant To Servicemembers Civil Relief Act is granted. If defendant is in military service, the court must stay proceedings for at least 90 days if the court determines there may be a defense to the action that requires defendant's presence [See 50 USC App. 521(d), 522(b)]. The court may extend that stay for up to the duration of defendant's military service plus 90 days upon a showing that military duty continues to affect defendant's ability to appear. [See 50 USC App. 521(d), 525(a)]. Here, Defendant Michael Taylor has been on active duty since April 17, 2015 and will be on active duty for a period of 425 days (June 15, 2016). Therefore the court finds the stay is appropriate for a period of Defendant Taylor's entire deployment, plus a period of 90 days thereafter. The proceedings shall be stayed until September 13, 2016 (June 15, 2016, plus 90 days). Further, the court finds Defendant Michael Taylor to be a key defendant whose interests are intertwined with all other Defendants, therefore the stay shall extend to all parties to avoid materially prejudicing all parties. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
